raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.0,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.3,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.0,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.2","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"The delegation explicitly asks to check the argument chain for logical consistency, making this the central responsibility.","RX.1":"Any projection of logical consistency should preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the provided text alone.","RX.2":"The projection should avoid expanding the task into broader validity, novelty, or empirical adequacy judgments.","RX.3":"The delegation is narrow, so maintaining the boundary around logical consistency is load-bearing.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid encouraging unsupported conclusions that could mislead downstream paper revision or review decisions.","RX.5":"The responsibility assignment should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact's argumentative structure."}}

rationale.R1.2: The delegation explicitly asks to check the argument chain for logical consistency, making this the central responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of logical consistency should preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the provided text alone.
rationale.RX.2: The projection should avoid expanding the task into broader validity, novelty, or empirical adequacy judgments.
rationale.RX.3: The delegation is narrow, so maintaining the boundary around logical consistency is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid encouraging unsupported conclusions that could mislead downstream paper revision or review decisions.
rationale.RX.5: The responsibility assignment should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact's argumentative structure.
